question_text: Which statement correctly describes the relationship between voltage, current, and resistance in a circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: If voltage increases and resistance stays the same, the current will decrease.

options.label: B
options.text: If voltage increases and resistance stays the same, the current will increase.

options.label: C
options.text: Current and voltage are not related to each other in a circuit.

options.label: D
options.text: If resistance increases, the current will increase even if voltage stays the same.

explanation: According to Ohm's law (V = I × R), current is directly proportional to voltage when resistance is constant. Increasing voltage pushes more charge through the circuit, increasing current. Options A and D incorrectly reverse this relationship, and option C ignores the fundamental link between voltage and current.
